Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.","type":"number"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"},"name":"add_comment"},{"description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. 2026-03-13T08:28:29.8218218Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-13T08:28:11.398631318Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** This is a feature request / bug fix for the Azure Generator (`eng/packages/http-client-csharp`). When multiple pageable operations in a TypeSpec definition share the same generated `CollectionResult` type name, the generator produces a name collision error. The issue requests that the generator detect and resolve such collisions automatically.\n\nThe issue is filed by a repo member (@ArcturusZhang) and already has the appropriate `Client` and `CodeGen` labels applied.\n\n---\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Technical Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n### Root Cause\n\nThe `CollectionResult` type name is derived algorithmically from the interface and operation names. Two distinct scenarios can produce a collision:\n\n1. **Same interface, different operations** — e.g., `Foo.list()` and `Foo.listAll()` may both produce `FooCollectionResult` if the name-derivation logic strips common prefixes like `list`.\n2. **Cross-interface concatenation ambiguity** — e.g., `Foo.barBaz()` → `FooBarBaz` and `FooBar.baz()` → `FooBarBaz` are identical, since the type name is formed by concatenation of interface + operation name components.\n\n### Affected Area\n\n- Generator code responsible for naming `CollectionResult` types (likely in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/src/`)\n- Look for the naming/provider class that builds pageable result type names.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔍 Suggested Debugging / Reproduction\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nMinimal TypeSpec repros that should trigger the collision:\n\n**Case 1 — same interface:**\n```typespec\ninterface Foo {\n  op list(...): Page\u003cItem\u003e;\n  op listAll(...): Page\u003cItem\u003e;\n}\n```\n\n**Case 2 — cross-interface ambiguity:**\n```typespec\ninterface Foo {\n  op barBaz(...): Page\u003cItem\u003e;\n}\ninterface FooBar {\n  op baz(...): Page\u003cItem\u003e;\n}\n```\n\nSteps:\n1. Add one of the above TypeSpec snippets to a test spec under `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/`.\n2. Run `npm install \u0026\u0026 npm run build` in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp`.\n3. Run `./eng/packages/http-client-csharp/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1 -filter \u003ctest-name\u003e`.\n4. Observe the name collision error in the generated output.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Implementation Checklist\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Identify where `CollectionResult` type names are constructed in the generator (likely a `Provider` or `Visitor` class in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/src/`)\n- [ ] Add a name-uniqueness check: after all `CollectionResult` names are derived, detect duplicates\n- [ ] Define a disambiguation strategy (e.g., append a numeric suffix, use full qualified operation name, or fail early with a descriptive error message pointing to the TypeSpec source)\n- [ ] Add a test TypeSpec spec (both Case 1 and Case 2 above) that exercises the fix\n- [ ] Run `./eng/packages/http-client-csharp/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` to validate regenerated test projects pass\n- [ ] Run `npm run lint \u0026\u0026 npm run prettier` in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔗 Relevant Resources\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [`eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/src/`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/src) — Azure Generator source\n- CODEOWNERS for this path: @JoshLove-msft @m-nash @jorgerangel-msft @live1206 @ArcturusZhang @jsquire @christothes\n\n\u003c/details\u003e","item_number":57063},"name":"add_comment"}}}
